title: Amendment to Executive Order 13603: Modification of Defense Production Act Authority Delegations
summary: This Executive Order amends Executive Order 13603 from March 16, 2012, which addresses National Defense Resources Preparedness. The order modifies certain delegations of presidential authority under the Defense Production Act of 1950. The specific adjustments to these delegations are detailed within the order's provisions, affecting how presidential powers granted under the Defense Production Act are distributed among executive branch departments and agencies.

title: Executive Order on Advertising Standards for Products Labeled as Made in America
summary: This executive order establishes a policy regarding the advertising and labeling of products marketed as Made in America. The order recognizes that consumers have a right to receive clear, accurate, substantiated, and accessible information about whether products advertised with Made in America claims are actually manufactured in the United States. The order aims to protect American consumers by establishing standards for truthful advertising practices related to country of origin claims on consumer products.

title: Executive Order on Reducing Regulatory Requirements for Residential Housing Development
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to identify and remove regulatory provisions determined to create barriers to the construction and renovation of residential housing. The order establishes that housing market dynamism depends on the ability to construct varied inventory of new homes and undertake renovation projects. The order instructs relevant agencies to review existing regulations with the stated purpose of reducing impediments to residential housing development and construction activities.

title: Executive Order on Mortgage Credit Access and Lending Standards
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to review and implement policies aimed at expanding access to mortgage credit for homebuyers across various credit profiles. The order establishes that mortgage lending should be available to Americans seeking to purchase homes at interest rates that correspond to their individual creditworthiness levels. The order addresses previous regulatory and policy frameworks affecting the mortgage lending market and directs relevant federal agencies to take actions designed to increase the availability of mortgage products and reduce barriers to homeownership financing.

title: Proclamation on Women's History Month 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation designating March 2026 as Women's History Month. The proclamation acknowledges the contributions of American women throughout the nation's history across various domains including family, culture, military service, industry, and national development. The proclamation recognizes both historical female figures and contemporary women leaders and their roles in American society and governance.

title: Presidential Nominations and Withdrawal Notices Submitted to Senate
summary: The President submitted nominations and withdrawal notices to the Senate on matters of executive personnel appointments. The submission includes a nomination of Sarah B. Rogers of New York for the position of Chief Executive Officer of the United States Agency for Global Media. Additionally, the submission includes withdrawal notices related to previously submitted nominations, including that of William Hewes III of Mississippi for a Commissioner position at the Consumer Product Safety Commission, a position with a term of seven years beginning October 27, 2024.

title: Presidential Memorandum Regarding Compensation Practices at the Tennessee Valley Authority
summary: This presidential memorandum directs the Board of Directors of the Tennessee Valley Authority to implement measures related to compensation practices. The memorandum is issued under the authority granted to the President by the Constitution and the Tennessee Valley Authority Act of 1933, as amended. The document addresses fiscal matters concerning how the TVA structures and manages employee compensation.

title: Presidential Nominations to the Senate for Cabinet and Federal Positions
summary: This document contains presidential nominations sent to the United States Senate for various federal positions. Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma has been nominated for the position of Secretary of Homeland Security. James Arnott of Missouri has been nominated to serve as United States Marshal for the Western District of Missouri for a four-year term. Jack Chambers of West Virginia has been nominated to serve as United States Marshal for the Southern District of West Virginia for a four-year term. These nominations require Senate confirmation before the individuals can assume their respective positions.

title: Proclamation Designating U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day, 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation establishing U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day, 2026. The proclamation acknowledges Americans held in captivity or wrongfully detained abroad and expresses the administration's stated commitment to their welfare and return. The document affirms the government's position regarding support for detained or captive U.S. citizens and outlines the observance of this designated day.

title: Presidential Proclamation on Countercartel and Counterterrorism Operations in the Western Hemisphere
summary: This presidential proclamation outlines the administration's policies and initiatives regarding the disruption of cartel operations and transnational criminal organizations in the Western Hemisphere. The proclamation documents designations of various cartels and transnational gangs as foreign terrorist organizations and describes the allocation of resources and coordination efforts directed toward countering their criminal activities. The proclamation articulates the federal government's approach to addressing organized crime and terrorism threats within the region through law enforcement, international cooperation, and related security measures.
